name: L

primary_sources.citation: G. Hurlbert, A Survey of Graph Pebbling, arXiv:math/0406024
primary_sources.quote: the Lemke graph L, whose vertex set is {a,b,c,d,w,x,y,z} and whose edge set consists of the union of the complete bipartite graphs {a}×{b,c,d} and {b,c,d}×{w,z} with the path w,x,y,z,a

primary_sources.citation: K. Wood, J. Pulaj, Bilevel Programming for Pebbling Numbers of Lemke Graph Products, arXiv:2411.19314

labelling_note: Vertices are 0..7 with bilevel-paper labels 1..8 mapped by v - 1. Hurlbert <-> bilevel isomorphism: a=5, b=4, c=6, d=8, w=3, x=2, y=1, z=7 (bilevel labels 1..8).

configuration_index_0based.comment: 0-based indices map: a=4, b=3, c=5, d=7, w=2, x=1, y=0, z=6

two_pebbling_witness.claim: From this 12-pebble configuration, two pebbles cannot be moved to root x.
